stages.name: unit_tests_failing_first
stages.evidence: Before implementation, task-specific tests exposed the audited failures: symlink-blind containment helper behavior, argv-unsafe generated same-file commands, missing backend parse-error file context, doctest report schema drift, invalid AST candidate diagnostics, snapshot helpers that could self-bless missing files, doctest AI command-evidence redaction gaps, and duplicate physical edits from the real comparison/loop-boundary AST overlap.

stages.name: unit_tests
stages.evidence: Full suite passed after implementation (533 tests).

stages.name: json_syntax_check
stages.command: python3 -m json.tool tasks/queue.json tasks/status.json tests/coverage-gaps/failure_modes.v1.json tests/coverage-gaps/invariants.v1.json tests/coverage-gaps/schemas.v1.json

stages.name: task_system_validation_active
stages.evidence: Validator passed with task 123 active after synchronizing task Markdown and queue JSON allowed files and using stable F-045 numbering for the new symlink-escape failure mode.

residual_risk: The shared containment helper is unit-tested against lexical escapes and symlinked components; most CLI call sites are covered by adapter code paths and docs rather than a separate integration test per flag.

Cleanup failures are surfaced as deterministic stderr warnings from the CLI adapter; no platform-specific forced-cleanup-failure fixture was added because reliably creating one without mutating unrelated filesystem state is brittle.
Result-cache reuse remains disabled; this task only hardens read/write containment and generated-command evidence before future reuse.
